decision_basis: A lineage-level result is eligible for load-bearing interpretation only when all predefined criteria pass: source and receiver terminal counts are each at least 25; source and receiver country counts are each at least 3; A_s > 1; D < 0; the equal-n rarefaction upper bound remains below 0; the geographic permutation test gives p <= 0.05; and the preassigned lineage role is neither support-only nor boundary.
figure2_inset_role: Defines the strict promotion gate for Figure 2 readers; directional compatibility alone is not sufficient for a promoted manuscript claim.
interpretation_boundary: This inset is decision-grammar context only; it does not classify geographic origin, ethnic identity, population continuity, migration route, or population-level probability.

decision_basis: A comparison is retained as support-only or boundary evidence when it is directionally compatible, historically relevant, or diagnostically informative but does not satisfy the full load-bearing eligibility gate after branch-unit reliability, role, or scope checks.
figure2_inset_role: Shows how compatible-looking or relevant comparisons are prevented from becoming load-bearing claims when reliability or scope criteria are not met.
interpretation_boundary: This inset is decision-grammar context only; support or boundary assignment should not be read as validation, model failure, or historical-origin evidence.

decision_basis: No source-receiver conclusion is drawn when source-side representation is insufficient, source and receiver masks overlap, the lineage root is too broad, or the available layer lacks the geography-bearing terminal evidence needed for the stated test.
source_terms_from_table3: No-call: insufficient source-side representation; No-call: overlapping source and receiver masks; No-call: broad lineage root; Topology-only layer; Receiver-side regional layer
figure2_inset_role: Explains why parts of the broad atlas remain no-call or deferred rather than being forced into compatible or rejected claims.
interpretation_boundary: This inset is decision-grammar context only; no-call or deferred status is not direct negative evidence and is not evidence of geographic origin, ethnic identity, population continuity, or migration route.
